video_summary: This video explores the evolution of software deployment, from manual processes to automated CI/CD pipelines, discussing key tools like Jenkins and GitHub Actions, and detailing various deployment strategies including manual approval, continuous delivery, and progressive rollouts.

recommended_sequence.description: The sequential process of developing software, which typically involves writing new code, merging it with the existing codebase, thorough testing, and finally deploying the application to users.
recommended_sequence.reason: 'Software Development Workflow' is a root concept (no prerequisites) and must be understood first because it directly enables: Software Testing, Software Deployment, CI/CD (Continuous Integration/Continuous Delivery), Manual Software Development Process.

recommended_sequence.description: An application designed to handle increasing workloads or user bases over time, necessitating continuous changes, bug fixes, and feature additions rather than a one-time build.
recommended_sequence.reason: 'Scalable Application' is an isolated foundational concept with no prerequisites and no dependents.

recommended_sequence.description: The complete collection of source code files that comprise a particular software application or project.
recommended_sequence.reason: 'Codebase' is an isolated foundational concept with no prerequisites and no dependents.

recommended_sequence.description: Defects or flaws in software that cause it to behave unexpectedly or incorrectly, often leading to malfunctions or crashes.
recommended_sequence.reason: 'Errors / Bugs' is an isolated foundational concept with no prerequisites and no dependents.

recommended_sequence.description: The process of evaluating software to identify bugs, errors, or any deviations from expected behavior, ensuring quality before deployment.
recommended_sequence.reason: 'Software Testing' builds upon Software Development Workflow (importance 4/10) and is required before studying: Software Deployment.

recommended_sequence.description: The traditional method where development, testing, and deployment steps are performed manually, often leading to increased human error, inconsistencies, and slower release cycles.
recommended_sequence.reason: 'Manual Software Development Process' builds upon Software Development Workflow (importance 6/10) and is a terminal concept in this learning path.

recommended_sequence.description: The process of making a software application available for use by end-users, involving tasks like releasing, configuring, and enabling the software on servers.
recommended_sequence.reason: 'Software Deployment' builds upon Software Development Workflow, Software Testing (importance 7/10) and is required before studying: CI/CD (Continuous Integration/Continuous Delivery), Manual Deployment Approval, Canary Deployment / Progressive Delivery.

recommended_sequence.description: A set of practices in software development that automates the integration, testing, and deployment of code changes, aiming to deliver software reliably and frequently.
recommended_sequence.reason: 'CI/CD (Continuous Integration/Continuous Delivery)' builds upon Software Development Workflow, Software Deployment (importance 9/10) and is required before studying: CI/CD Automation Tools, Continuous Delivery (CD).

recommended_sequence.description: A step in the deployment process where human authorization is required before a software change can be released, typically used for critical systems or highly sensitive changes.
recommended_sequence.reason: 'Manual Deployment Approval' builds upon Software Deployment (importance 6/10) and is a terminal concept in this learning path.

recommended_sequence.description: A deployment strategy that releases a new version of software to a small subset of users first. If the new version performs well without issues, it's gradually rolled out to more users, minimizing risk.
recommended_sequence.reason: 'Canary Deployment / Progressive Delivery' builds upon Software Deployment (importance 8/10) and is a terminal concept in this learning path.

recommended_sequence.description: Software platforms designed to automate the various stages of a CI/CD pipeline, including building, testing, and deploying applications.
recommended_sequence.reason: 'CI/CD Automation Tools' builds upon CI/CD (Continuous Integration/Continuous Delivery) (importance 8/10) and is required before studying: Jenkins, GitHub Actions.

recommended_sequence.description: A software engineering approach where teams produce software in short cycles, ensuring that the software can be released reliably at any time, though actual deployment to production may still require a manual trigger or approval.
recommended_sequence.reason: 'Continuous Delivery (CD)' builds upon CI/CD (Continuous Integration/Continuous Delivery) (importance 7/10) and is a terminal concept in this learning path.

recommended_sequence.description: An open-source automation server widely used for CI/CD, acting as a jobs orchestrator to automate software building, testing, and deployment. It's known for its extensibility but can be complex to maintain.
recommended_sequence.reason: 'Jenkins' builds upon CI/CD Automation Tools (importance 9/10) and is required before studying: Jobs Orchestrator.

recommended_sequence.description: A feature from GitHub that allows automation of software workflows, including CI/CD, directly within a GitHub repository, often seen as a modern alternative to Jenkins.
recommended_sequence.reason: 'GitHub Actions' builds upon CI/CD Automation Tools (importance 7/10) and is a terminal concept in this learning path.

recommended_sequence.description: A system or tool responsible for scheduling, managing, and executing various automated tasks or 'jobs' in a defined sequence, common in CI/CD pipelines.
recommended_sequence.reason: 'Jobs Orchestrator' builds upon Jenkins (importance 5/10) and is a terminal concept in this learning path.

Study after completing the previous level.
